alerts.title: Iraqi militias, Hezbollah threaten U.S. bases amid escalating proxy attacks
alerts.summary: Iran-backed Iraqi Shiite militias and Hezbollah issued threats to attack U.S. military bases in Iraq if Washington joins Iranian strikes. Drone attacks on U.S. bases in Iraq confirmed by Al Jazeera and BBC (reporting 28 attacks in single day on one base). Iraqi government summoned U.S. diplomat. This signals rapid proxy force escalation coordinated with Iran's kinetic campaign. Source: incidents feed (multiple outlets including NYT, BBC, Al Jazeera).

alerts.title: U.S. deploys submarine to Persian Gulf as threat level reaches 8/10 SEVERE
alerts.summary: USS Florida submarine deployed to Persian Gulf (26.29°N, 52.35°E) per assets feed. Concurrent with threat level rising to 8/10 (SEVERE — ONGOING ESCALATION) and three carrier battle groups now forward-deployed (Eisenhower in Red Sea, Lincoln in Arabian Sea, Ford in Mediterranean). Submarine deployment indicates preparation for undersea contingency operations in critical chokepoint.

alerts.title: Houthis impose total naval blockade on Israeli shipping across Red Sea
alerts.summary: Yemen's Iran-backed Houthis declared total ban on Israeli vessels in Red Sea and vow to attack Israel-affiliated ships, per 14+ independent sources (Reuters, Jerusalem Post, Al Arabiya, Al Jazeera, others) clustered in single reporting cycle. Hezbollah statement confirms coordination: 'Houthis join Iran war fight.' Dual blockade threat now extends from Strait of Hormuz (Iran) to Bab el-Mandeb (Houthis), choking two critical maritime corridors. Source: incidents feed.

alerts.title: Israel continues strikes despite Trump de-escalation call; fractured messaging
alerts.summary: Rybar (~78K views) reports Israel launched additional airstrikes deep inside Iran on June 8, even after Trump told Netanyahu 'war will soon end' and called for de-escalation. Iranian officials claim Hormuz blockade now active and supported by Houthis per Rybar (~122K views, date-flagged). Signal of coalition fracture: U.S. seeks pause; Israel/Iran escalation cycle continuing unabated. Breakdown of unified Western messaging on conflict termination.

alerts.title: U.S. domestic sentiment sharply negative; Iran/Israel dominate news cycle
alerts.summary: U.S. sentiment analysis shows 53% negative vs. 38% positive (15-point negative swing). Word frequency dominated by 'Trump' (88 mentions), 'Iran' (17), 'Netanyahu' (14), 'Israel' (10), 'war' (8). Domestic political crisis (judge, Senate, election, attorney terms prevalent) intertwined with conflict escalation, indicating deepening polarization and distraction from strategic coordination. Sentiment shift suggests erosion of domestic consensus for Middle East policy. Source: sentiment feed.

analysis_note: Iran and Israel remain locked in active kinetic exchange with announced operational pauses but no ceasefire; proxy forces (Houthis, Iraqi militias, Hezbollah) escalating in parallel; U.S. military presence maximal (3 carriers, submarine, Red Sea task force); nuclear breakout window 1-2 weeks; oil at $94.37/bbl; threat level 8/10; Trump-Netanyahu messaging rift widening amid continued Israeli operations.
